content.text: A
transformer block

repeats two sublayers: multi-head self-attention and a position-wise feed-forward network (FFN). The FFN applies the same MLP to each token independently, adding non-linear capacity. Stacking many blocks—attention for mixing, FFN for processing—is what turns raw embeddings into the representations agent models use for tool selection and language.
What it is
Full transformer block
One block (pre-norm variant common in LLMs):

x = x + SelfAttn(LayerNorm(x))
x = x + FFN(LayerNorm(x))
Feed-forward sublayer
For each token vector
Typically
expands to
is GELU or SwiGLU, then
projects back. SwiGLU uses gated linear units:
Why it matters
Attention mixes information between tokens; FFN
transforms each token's vector locally

. Without FFN depth, the model would mostly re-weight existing features. Agent behaviors—mapping natural language to strict JSON schemas, applying policy rules—depend on deep FFN stacks after attention has gathered context.
Benefits
Large MLP inner dimension increases model capacity and parameter count efficiently
Per-token parallelism—FFN is
Residual connections let gradients flow through deep stacks (dozens of blocks)
Trade-offs
FFN layers hold most parameters in many LLMs (especially with SwiGLU)
MoE replaces dense FFN with routed experts—better scale but complex inference
Depth increases latency linearly with layer count during decode
Performance and scalability

Inference optimizes FFN matmuls heavily (quantization, fusion). For agents, time-to-first-token scales with prompt length and layers; tokens-per-second during tool argument streaming scales with FFN and attention at each decode step.
Mental model
Attention is a meeting where tokens share notes; FFN is each token thinking privately about what to do with those notes before the next meeting.
Key takeaways
Transformer block = attention + FFN, each with residual + norm
FFN is position-wise MLP, typically 4× expansion
Depth (number of blocks) drives model capability and inference cost
Agent LLM quality scales with stack depth and width, not attention alone
